title: Our Warming Earth
passage:
Our Warming Earth

Our planet, Earth, is getting warmer. This is called global warming. Scientists say that the temperature of the Earth is going up every year. This happens because of greenhouse gases. These gases are in the air around the Earth. They act like a warm blanket. They trap the heat from the sun and keep the planet warm. But now, there are too many greenhouse gases in the air, so the Earth is becoming too hot.

Why do we have so many greenhouse gases? One big reason is that people burn fossil fuels. Fossil fuels are coal, oil, and natural gas. We burn them to make electricity, drive cars, and run factories. When we burn these fuels, they release dirty gas into the air. Another reason is that people cut down too many trees. Forests and trees are very important because they clean the air. They take in bad gases and give us clean oxygen. Without trees, the air gets dirtier and warmer.

Global warming changes our weather and our planet. In very cold places, like the North Pole and South Pole, the ice is melting fast. Because the ice melts, the water in the oceans is rising. This can cause floods in cities near the sea. The weather is also changing. Many places have very hot summers, and some places have big storms and heavy rain.

But we can help to stop global warming. We can do small things every day. First, we can save energy. We should turn off lights and computers when we do not use them. Second, we can walk or ride a bicycle instead of driving a car. This keeps the air clean. Finally, we can plant more trees in our gardens and parks. If we work together, we can protect our beautiful Earth.

questions.text: What acts like a warm blanket around the Earth?
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'This happens because of greenhouse gases... They act like a warm blanket.'

questions.explanation: The passage states: 'We burn them to make electricity, drive cars, and run factories.'

questions.text: What is happening to the ice in the North Pole and South Pole?
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'In very cold places, like the North Pole and South Pole, the ice is melting fast.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: The Earth is getting warmer every year.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Scientists say that the temperature of the Earth is going up every year.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Trees take in bad gases and give us clean oxygen.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'They take in bad gases and give us clean oxygen.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Rising ocean water can cause floods in cities near the sea.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Because the ice melts, the water in the oceans is rising. This can cause floods in cities near the sea.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Most scientists live in the North Pole.
questions.explanation: The text mentions scientists and the North Pole, but it does not say where scientists live.

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Greenhouse gases trap the ______ from the sun.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'They trap the heat from the sun and keep the planet warm.'

questions.text: Fossil fuels include coal, oil, and ______.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Fossil fuels are coal, oil, and natural gas.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Without trees, the air gets dirtier and ______.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Without trees, the air gets dirtier and warmer.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: To keep the air clean, we can walk or ride a ______.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Second, we can walk or ride a bicycle instead of driving a car.'

vocabulary.meaning: the process of the Earth's temperature rising
gases in the atmosphere that trap heat
natural fuels like coal, oil, and gas
to turn from ice/solid into water/liquid
to keep something safe from harm

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

Để đạt điểm cao ở bài đọc này, hãy áp dụng các mẹo sau:

  • Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Từ vựng trong câu hỏi mức độ A1-A2 rất sát với bài đọc. Hãy khoanh tròn từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'blanket', 'fossil fuels', 'North Pole') rồi tìm đúng từ đó trong bài đọc.
  • Chú ý giới hạn từ: Với dạng bài điền từ (Sentence Completion), hãy đọc kỹ yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS'. Không viết quá 2 từ.
  • Nhận biết NOT GIVEN: Nếu trong câu hỏi có các thông tin không hề được nhắc đến trong văn bản (ví dụ như nơi sống của nhà khoa học), hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN.
